coreIssue: This bi-weekly check-in should test whether Lin’s recent late-night pace as Head of Product was a one-off launch push or an unhealthy operating pattern.
openingQuestion: What is actually driving your workload right now that the launches are behind you, and which parts of it only make sense for you to keep personally?
listenFor: whether he names a specific recurring responsibility or stakeholder pull that is still consuming evenings
whether he gives concrete examples of work he is holding because of judgment, speed, or trust concerns
whether he minimizes the load quickly or, if pressed, points to a recent week, project, or decision bottleneck
avoid: do not lead with praise for the launches and then soften the workload question until the real pattern stays vague
do not jump straight into telling a senior Head of Product what to delegate before you hear where decision authority or team autonomy may be stuck
goodOutcome: You and Lin have agreed what lead-level Head of Product work he should personally own this cycle and one category of work that should move off his plate now.
suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick one concrete example from the past three weeks where Lin was working late and use it to anchor the conversation if he stays general.
